summary.intent: Implement a cron-based follow-up engine (CHA-35) that periodically queries the CRM for stale contacts and triggers an agent notification, mirroring the existing heartbeat registration pattern.
summary.outcome: All 7 planned files were implemented (FollowUpConfig in schema/validate/template, contacts_needing_followup in CRM store, follow_up.rs prompt builder in gateway, server.rs registration), 14 tests added and passing, clippy clean, formatted with pinned nightly, and committed.

repo: System cron jobs follow an idempotent upsert pattern in server.rs using a fixed ID like __heartbeat__ or __follow_up__, removed on next startup when disabled
PII fields on Contact are wrapped in Secret<String>; the convention is to never expose these to LLM prompts — use a StaleContactSummary projection type instead
CRM store uses a trait-based abstraction (CrmStore) with SQLite and Memory implementations; new query methods go in the trait with a default fallback impl for MemoryStore

The on_agent_turn callback in server.rs is where session-type-specific enrichment happens (like heartbeat events); follow-up prompt enrichment follows the same detect-then-enrich pattern using SessionTarget::Named
DRY extraction of system job registration helpers is intentionally deferred until a third system job appears — two instances (heartbeat + follow-up) is not enough to justify abstraction
The retention engine (retention.rs) uses a detached tokio::spawn loop rather than the CronService — this is an acknowledged anti-pattern but out of scope to change
Config defaults to disabled (follow_up.enabled = false) making it a zero-risk deploy with no migrations required

code.finding: Heartbeat job upsert pattern — the exact template for __follow_up__ registration including idempotent add/update/remove logic
Interaction.created_at is a public field, allowing test setup to backdate interactions to simulate staleness
contacts_needing_followup uses a single LEFT JOIN SQL query to avoid N+1; results capped at 50 to prevent OOM on large CRM datasets

workflow: The autonomous agent pattern: compile-check first, then read all relevant files before writing any code, commit only after full quality suite passes
Tests T4-T6 (SQLite stale contact query) and T1-T3 (FollowUpConfig serde/defaults) were missing after initial implementation and had to be added in a follow-up pass
Using a Linear comment as a persistent workpad with milestone updates keeps context across agent runs without relying on conversation history
Pinned nightly rustfmt (not stable) is required for formatting — must use the workspace's pinned toolchain, not system default

friction: Tests T1-T6 were not written during the initial implementation pass and had to be discovered missing and added separately, suggesting the agent wrote feature code before test code
Multiple grep/read passes were needed to locate existing test modules in store_sqlite.rs and validate.rs before tests could be appended — the files are large with tests at the bottom
The transcript ends before showing the git push and PR creation, so it's unclear if the PR was successfully created and linked to the Linear issue
open_items: Stage-aware follow-up thresholds (different stale_days per contact lifecycle stage: Lead=7, Prospect=14, Active=30)
Autonomous outreach — agent drafting or sending follow-up messages directly to contacts (deferred: legal/compliance concerns)
Follow-up outcome tracking — new CRM interaction type to record that a follow-up was sent
FOLLOW_UP.md prompt file — user-customizable prompt template mirroring HEARTBEAT.md
Retention engine migration to CronService — currently a detached tokio::spawn, acknowledged anti-pattern but separate concern
Extract system_jobs.rs module — if a third system cron job is added, extract heartbeat + follow-up registration into a shared module
T14 (job updated when config changes) integration test was listed in engineering review spec but not confirmed as written in the transcript
